name: Transmission
short_desc: Transmission BitTorrent client routed through a self-hosted WireGuard VPN gateway with kill-switch and port forwarding.
author: tenequm
description: Transmission BitTorrent client whose traffic is forced through a self-hosted WireGuard VPN gateway (gluetun in custom mode). The gluetun container holds the network namespace and enforces a hard kill-switch: if the tunnel drops, no traffic leaks. Inbound peer connections arrive via a port forwarded on the gateway, giving Transmission a real open port (good download speed and seeding ratio) while egress exits through the gateway IP. Designed to pair with a WireGuard gateway that forwards the peer port back over the tunnel.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Private Key
form_fields.hint: Client private key for the VPN gateway tunnel (the peer the gateway is configured to accept).

form_fields.label: WireGuard Server Public Key
form_fields.hint: Public key of the VPN gateway's WireGuard interface.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Endpoint IP
form_fields.hint: Public IP of your VPN gateway.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Endpoint Port
form_fields.hint: UDP port the gateway's WireGuard interface listens on.

form_fields.label: WireGuard Tunnel Address
form_fields.hint: This client's address inside the tunnel.

form_fields.label: BitTorrent Peer Port
form_fields.hint: Must match the port forwarded on the gateway and Transmission's peer-port in settings.json.

form_fields.label: Allowed LAN / Docker Subnets
form_fields.hint: Comma-separated subnets allowed to bypass the kill-switch (LAN + Runtipi docker network) so the web UI and *arr apps can reach Transmission.
